title: Crispy Spicy Mediterranean Chicken Bowl
description: Zesty, FODMAPfriendly bowls of slowcooked chicken paired with crisp corn tortillas, fresh veggies, and a citrus yogurt drizzle, perfect for a hangover cure.
instructions: Season the chicken thighs with smoked paprika, cumin, chili powder, lemon zest, salt, and half the olive oil; set aside 10 minutes to absorb flavors.
Place the chicken in a slow‑cooker, add the remaining olive oil, and slow‑cook on low for 6–7 hours until the meat is fall‑apart tender.
While the chicken cooks, warm the corn tortillas on a dry skillet until crisp and slightly charred, about 30 seconds per side; set aside.
In a small bowl, whisk together lemon juice, chopped parsley, and yogurt to create a creamy citrus sauce.
When the chicken is done, shred it with two forks directly in the slow‑cooker; mix in the lemon‑yogurt sauce and adjust seasoning if needed.
Divide the shredded chicken among four bowls, top each with diced cucumber, tomato, and the crispy tortillas.
Garnish with the fresh green onion tops and a drizzle of the citrus sauce.
Serve immediately, or let cool slightly before eating to help soothe hangover symptoms.
